personal.name: Sarthak Agrawal
personal.title: Software Engineer
personal.subtitle: Full Stack Developer · Web Dev · ML Enthusiast · Competitive Programmer
personal.bio: I'm a final-year Computer Engineering student at SGSITS, Indore, with a deep passion for building things on the web. I've been coding since school — competitive programming got me hooked, and full-stack development kept me here. I enjoy the entire process: from sketching out an idea to shipping something real that people actually use.

education.institution: Shri G.S. Institute of Technology and Science, Indore

experience.description: Working as a software engineer intern, overhauling frontend UI components and building automated data pipelines.
responsibilities: Overhauled frontend UI components, enforcing validation rules and building a client-side error-handling layer that improved data integrity and reduced user-facing errors.
Automated data collection from dynamic websites using Puppeteer, replacing manual workflows and enabling scalable, repeatable data pipelines.
Analyzed large-scale web workflow datasets using Python to surface actionable insights that informed internal product decisions.

experience.description: Developed a responsive website for GoMediafy showcasing their digital marketing services, client case studies and testimonials.
responsibilities: Developed a responsive website for GoMediafy showcasing their digital marketing services, client case studies and testimonials.
Architected a CMS-driven back end enabling non-technical staff to add or update service pages and case studies on the fly.

projects.title: Intrusion Detection System for SDN
projects.shortDescription: ML-driven Intrusion Detection System in a virtual SDN environment with real-time traffic analysis and attack classification.
projects.guide: Dr. Urjita Thakar, CSE Department, SGSITS
projects.fullDescription: Currently developing a machine learning–driven Intrusion Detection System within a virtual SDN environment using Mininet and the Ryu Controller. The system integrates real-time traffic analysis and attack classification for DoS, DDoS, and Probe attacks through Scikit-learn, enhancing network visibility and automated threat detection capabilities.
keyFeatures: Real-time network traffic monitoring using Mininet virtual environment
Attack classification for DoS, DDoS, and Probe attack types
Integration with Ryu SDN Controller via OpenFlow protocol
ML-based anomaly detection using Scikit-learn algorithms
Network traffic analysis and visualization using Wireshark
projects.challenges: Simulating realistic network traffic patterns in a virtual environment while maintaining high detection accuracy across different attack vectors.
projects.outcome: Enhanced network security posture with automated, real-time threat detection and classification capabilities within the SDN environment.

projects.title: Human Activity Recognition Model
projects.shortDescription: Deep learning model to classify human activities from video sequences using TensorFlow/Keras and the UCF50 dataset.
projects.guide: Mrs. Himani Mishra, CSE Department, SGSITS
projects.fullDescription: Developed a machine learning model using TensorFlow/Keras trained on the UCF50 dataset to classify human actions from video sequences. The model identifies various activities such as walking, cycling, and playing instruments, with a strong focus on optimizing accuracy and generalization across diverse real-world scenarios.
keyFeatures: Video sequence processing using OpenCV for efficient frame extraction
Deep learning model architecture built with TensorFlow/Keras
Trained on UCF50 benchmark dataset with 50 distinct action categories
Optimized for generalization to prevent overfitting
Automated dataset pipeline using yt-dlp for video downloads
projects.challenges: Handling large-scale video datasets efficiently while preventing overfitting on the diverse 50-class action categories from UCF50.
projects.outcome: Successfully built and trained a model that classifies multiple human activities with strong accuracy on the UCF50 benchmark dataset.

projects.title: Mess Management System
projects.shortDescription: Full-stack mess management platform built with Agile/Scrum methodology featuring digital tokens, subscriptions, and multi-role support.
projects.fullDescription: Collaboratively designed and developed a comprehensive mess management system using Agile/Scrum methodology. The system addresses key functionalities including menu management, digital token systems, subscription plans, feedback submission, and event planning. Supports multiple user roles including students, faculty, and mess managers with role-based access control.
keyFeatures: Digital token system for streamlined meal management and tracking
Multi-role support: students, faculty, and mess managers with RBAC
Dynamic menu management with real-time updates
Subscription plans and billing management module
Feedback submission and event planning modules
Fully responsive design optimized for mobile and desktop
projects.challenges: Coordinating multiple user roles with different permissions and workflows while maintaining a seamless, intuitive user experience across all roles.
projects.outcome: Delivered a scalable, full-featured mess management solution that streamlines day-to-day operations for educational institutions.
